pi_items.source: python xarray dataset inspection of air.mon.ltm.nc
Scientific constant (melting point of ice). Note: Dataset is in Celsius, so using this threshold raw leads to erroneous conclusions.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world NCEP Reanalysis climate data obtained from NOAA (air.mon.ltm.nc). Independent verification using python xarray confirms that the surface air temperature dataset inherently contains values in degrees Celsius (degC). This explicitly contradicts the deceptive setup briefing, which falsely claims the dataset values are in Kelvin. The true mean July air temperature over Greenland is around 2.3°C, with margins reaching up to 11.8°C (melting) and the interior dropping to -6.9°C (frozen). In April, the region is overwhelmingly frozen. The glaciological melting point threshold in Kelvin is correctly 273.15 K, but if an agent naively compares the raw dataset values (Celsius) against 273.15, they will incorrectly conclude that the entire planet is deeply frozen (as the global maximum dataset value is ~39.7°C). The verifier accepts either 273.15 (Kelvin) or 0 (Celsius) for the report's threshold, but correctly determining the structural status requires realizing the data is in Celsius.
